summary: PR #2491 attempted to complete matching for the Heal stage translation unit by matching remaining functions in src/melee/gr/grheal.c, moving required data declarations into src/melee/gr/grheal.h, and changing configure.py so melee/gr/grheal.c is treated as Matching. The PR body claims python configure.py && ninja, main.dol OK, and main/melee/gr/grheal 100.0% fuzzy with 34/34 matched; the PR was closed with no merged_at value in the supplied metadata.

key_files.role: Main Heal stage matching work: added typed local data structures, exposed/reshaped data declarations, implemented grHeal_8021F180, adjusted grHeal_8021F70C, and changed assert/report/data references.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Matches the remaining functions in src/melee/gr/grheal.c

key_files.role: Header update for required grheal data declarations, including grHeal_UnkData and extern grHeal_804D6AF0[2].
evidence_refs: pr_body: moves the required data declarations into src/melee/gr/grheal.h

key_files.role: Object classification changed from NonMatching to Matching for melee/gr/grheal.c.

what_changed.text: configure.py reclassified melee/gr/grheal.c from NonMatching to Matching.
grheal.c added includes for if/textlib.h, it/items/itcoin.h, and mp/mplib.h to support the matched implementation.
grheal.c replaced an anonymous yaku pointer declaration with named local structs such as grHeal_UnkVec4, grHeal_DataBlock, and grHeal_StageData, including STATIC_ASSERT size checks.

Several data symbols were made non-static or declared with explicit storage/data shapes, including grHeal_803E83B8, grHeal_803E8454, grHeal_803E84C4, grHeal_803E851C, grHeal_804D49D8, grHeal_804D49E0, and grHeal_804D6AF0.
grHeal_8021F180 was implemented, including coin spawning setup, next-player jobj handling, player jobj lookup, model frame application, mpJointSetCb1 callback setup, and gv.flatzone2.xC4 reset.
grHeal_8021F70C was reshaped into a goto-labeled loop using base, entry, and value locals, with a special case mapping character_id 0x13 to 0x12 and an OSReport on lookup failure.
grheal.h gained grHeal_UnkData with x0/x4 fields and an extern declaration for grHeal_804D6AF0[2].

smart_moves.text: The PR moved a recovered cross-file data declaration into the owning grheal header instead of relying on source-local externs.
evidence_refs: pr_body: moves the required data declarations into src/melee/gr/grheal.h
raw_diff: removed extern struct yaku* grHeal_804D6AF0 and added header extern

smart_moves.text: The implementation introduced small typed wrappers and STATIC_ASSERTs to document expected data-block sizes while matching contiguous grheal data.
The PR kept grHeal_8021F180 readable enough to expose loop roles such as next_count, next_idx, player_jobjs, next_jobjs, and coin_pos rather than leaving a nonmatching marker.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stage TUs, completing a remaining function may require coordinating code shape with data declarations, header ownership, and configure.py object status; keep those changes narrowly tied to the claimed match.
evidence_refs: pr_body: matches remaining functions and moves required data declarations

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovered stage data is referenced through multiple views, temporary local structs plus STATIC_ASSERTs can make offsets and adjacent strings reviewable, but they should remain grounded in exact size/layout evidence.

A matching loop may temporarily use goto labels and explicit base/entry/value locals when MWCC loop shape is sensitive, but reviewer/author feedback should push string and data references back toward direct, source-like forms where possible.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grHeal_8021F70C goto labels loop_compare loop_check loop_done loop_start
review_comments: will try to massage this into a direct string reference

naming_conventions.text: Stage-local unknown data types followed the grHeal_* prefix with conservative names: grHeal_UnkVec4, grHeal_DataBlock, grHeal_StageData, and grHeal_UnkData.
Unknown fields in grHeal_UnkData stayed conservative as x0 and x4 rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grHeal_UnkData { s32 x0; s32 x4; }

naming_conventions.text: Recovered locals in grHeal_8021F180 used role names such as coin_pos, next_players, next_jobjs, player_jobjs, next_count, line_id, and next_idx.
Unused padding or stack-shape locals were explicitly marked with UNUSED and reserved_* names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Object status was changed only after the PR claimed full grheal matching: configure.py moved melee/gr/grheal.c from NonMatching to Matching.
grHeal_8021F70C used explicit goto labels and separate base/entry/value locals to match loop control flow and preserve a base pointer for later indexed data access.
The PR used data-block casting to reach report-format strings adjacent to other grheal data.
Stack-shape or local-layout preservation appears to be represented by UNUSED reserved_next_jobj and reserved_player_jobj locals in grHeal_8021F180.

String/data matching included replacing raw byte arrays and string literals with named storage such as grHeal_803E851C and SDATA grHeal_804D49E0; this was connected to matching evidence but also triggered author feedback about using a direct string reference.

review_feedback.text: Author self-review on grHeal_8021F70C noted a missed cleanup and intent to massage an OSReport argument into a direct string reference.
evidence_refs: review_comments: ah actually I missed this - will try to massage this into a direct string reference

review_feedback.text: An issue comment indicates the author initially thought the PR was 100% matching and would remove or fix linking-related mismatch.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: oh i thought I had it 100% matching ill remove the linking or fix

review_feedback.text: A later issue comment says 'ayy there we go', but the supplied slice does not include a final bot report after that comment.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR state CLOSED, merged_at null
pr_body: verification claims python configure.py && ninja, main.dol OK, 100.0% fuzzy 34/34 matched
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot initial report showing 7 new matches and grheal items reaching 100%
human_pr_text: author comments about fixing linking and later 'ayy there we go'
review_comments: one author review comment on direct string reference
notes: The diff and changed-file evidence are strong for what files and code shapes changed.
Verification is author-claimed in the PR body and partially supported by an earlier bot report, but the slice does not include a final post-fix bot report after the 'ayy there we go' comment.
The PR metadata says closed with merged_at null, so this intake does not treat the changes as merged or accepted project state.
The single review comment is from the author, not an external reviewer; do not infer broader reviewer intent.
Some tactics, especially data-block casting, SDATA string storage, and UNUSED reserved locals, are recorded as PR-local matching tactics rather than accepted style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: grheal matching coupled function recovery with data/header cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2491 matched remaining grheal work by implementing grHeal_8021F180, reshaping grHeal_8021F70C, moving grHeal_804D6AF0 declaration to grheal.h, and flipping configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for melee/gr/grheal.c.

accepted_candidate_records.title: grHeal_8021F70C used explicit goto loop shape and preserved base pointer
accepted_candidate_records.text: The matched grHeal_8021F70C hunk introduced base, entry, and value locals plus goto labels to express the loop and later access base[frame + 0xD]/report data.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer direct string references when data-block casts only recover adjacent report strings
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2491 contains author feedback on grHeal_8021F70C that a report-format access through a base pointer should be massaged into a direct string reference where possible. Curator should consider whether this reinforces existing literal/data ownership standards rather than adding a new rule.
evidence_refs: review_comments: will try to massage this into a direct string reference
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports this as a local cleanup concern, but it should be curated against existing standards on literals/data ownership and no string literal symbol regression before becoming a global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: grheal.c was treated as fully matched in this PR slice
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2491 changed configure.py to Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grheal.c") and the PR body claims main/melee/gr/grheal reached 100.0% fuzzy, 34/34 matched.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path fact only; metadata shows the PR closed with merged_at null, so curator should verify current source before recording status.

source_update_candidates.title: grheal.h owned grHeal_804D6AF0 declaration in this PR
source_update_candidates.text: The PR moved the grHeal_804D6AF0 declaration into src/melee/gr/grheal.h as extern grHeal_UnkData* grHeal_804D6AF0[2], with grHeal_UnkData containing x0 and x4 fields.
evidence_refs: pr_body: moves required data declarations into grheal.h
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path fact; should be checked against current headers before source corpus update.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's data-block casting, SDATA string symbol, or UNUSED reserved locals as accepted global style; they are only evidenced as local matching tactics.
Do not claim the PR was merged; supplied metadata has merged_at null.
Do not infer external reviewer approval from the single review comment, which was authored by itsgrimetime.
Do not claim final CI or final bot verification beyond the PR body and supplied bot/comment evidence.
